page: Amazon Web Services r4.large is a memory-optimized, fourth-generation instance type. It features 2 vCPUs mapped to 1 physical Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 core with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The instance is equipped with 15.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 7.5 GB per vCPU. It runs on the Xen hypervisor under the x86_64 architecture. There is no local storage or GPU hardware bundled with this instance, and the baseline network bandwidth is rated at 1 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates that the r4.large sits in the lower performance tiers for compute-heavy and memory-throughput tasks. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU benchmarks rank in the bottom 10%, reflecting the older generation Intel Xeon architecture. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 77.72 GB/sec, but uncached large block read and write speeds drop to 8.31 GB/sec and 5.85 GB/sec respectively, both placing in the bottom 10%. PassMark memory performance is slightly better but remains weak at the bottom 25%.

The primary tradeoff of the r4.large is its reliance on legacy hardware, which limits its execution speed for modern workloads. For instance, Redis SET operations achieve 0.62 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput is measured at 1.52 GB/sec, both in the bottom 10%. However, the instance offers a high memory capacity relative to its vCPU count, making it qualitatively cost-effective for legacy memory-bound applications that do not require high CPU throughput. Appropriate workloads include small-scale caching layers, lightweight database operations, and legacy enterprise applications requiring a larger memory footprint without intensive computational demands.
description: Amazon Web Services r4.large is a memory-optimized, fourth-generation instance configured with 2 vCPUs and 15.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz on the Xen hypervisor, this x86_64 instance provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 7.5 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Performance benchmarks, including stress-ng and Geekbench, place the instance in the bottom 10% for compute and memory bandwidth, with uncached large-block memory read speeds at 8.31 GB/sec. Consequently, the r4.large represents a legacy tier with lower computational efficiency but high memory density. It is best suited for memory-bound workloads with low CPU requirements, such as small database operations, caching layers, and legacy enterprise applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r4.large is a memory-optimized instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 15.0 GB of RAM, and an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 CPU, designed for legacy memory-bound workloads with low compute demands.

tagline: A legacy memory-optimized instance offering high memory density for lightweight, memory-bound workloads and small-scale database operations.

Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Suited for lightweight caching and legacy memory-bound applications
